description: Agent Bank — precision-crafted agents forged via prompt-engineer methodology. Commander reads this registry to match tasks to agents automatically.

schema.name: Human-readable agent name
schema.model_tier: haiku | sonnet | opus — recommended model for this agent
schema.description: One-line description of what this agent does
schema.activation_triggers: Array of phrases/contexts that should activate this agent
schema.anti_triggers: Array of contexts where this agent should NOT be used
schema.input_spec: What the agent expects to receive
schema.pairs_with: Array of other agent IDs this agent works well alongside
schema.forged_date: ISO date when agent was created

agents.name: Code Architect
agents.description: Produces buildable system architectures from project briefs — component breakdown, API contracts, file structure, build order for agent task decomposition
activation_triggers: Design the architecture for a new project
Plan the system structure before building
User says how should I structure this or tech stack for X
Commander Step 3 (decompose) needs architectural decisions first
PRD provided that needs system design before coding begins
User asks about technology selections or component design
anti_triggers: Simple scripts or single-file tools — no architecture needed
Bug fixes or modifications to existing code
Content creation, research, or non-software tasks
Projects where architecture is already defined
agents.input_spec: Project brief, PRD, or natural language description of what to build. Optionally: constraints (team size, budget, timeline, deployment target).
agents.output_spec: Structured architecture document: technology selections with rationale, component breakdown with interfaces, data flow, file structure, API contracts, build order (parallelizable task graph), risks and trade-offs.
